ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for mochi-style or chocolate-frosted mochi donuts; closest parent 'doughnut food product' (FOODON_00002187) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Doughnuts) is the closest parent; no specific mochi donut subcategory exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for mochi-style chocolate donut; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Choco Mochimochi donut weighing approximately 65 g, as typically served at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Doughnuts, cake-type, chocolate-coated or frosted (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 18249); adapted with glutinous rice flour mochi composition reference from USDA Foundation Foods glutinous rice flour entry
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Choco Mochimochi is a mochi-style donut with a chocolate glaze sold by Dunkin' Donuts Singapore. No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods for this specific SKU. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy NDB 18249 (chocolate-frosted cake doughnut) as the primary structural analogue, with minor adjustments to reflect the higher glutinous rice flour content typical of mochi-style donuts (slightly lower protein, slightly higher carbohydrate). This is the closest defensible approved-source match available.

gi_source.title: Doughnut, cake-type — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to mochi-style chocolate donut
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Choco Mochimochi or mochi-style donuts in the University of Sydney GI Database. The GI of 76 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cake-type doughnuts (GI ~76, high), which is the closest structurally analogous food. Glutinous rice flour has a high GI (approximately 86–98 per University of Sydney data for glutinous rice), supporting that the mochi component does not lower the overall GI below the high category. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (76 × 33.3) / 100 ≈ 25.3, rounded to 25.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (338 mg per 100 g; approximately 220 mg per donut serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit consumption of Choco Mochimochi donuts, as a single serving contributes approximately 220 mg sodium — roughly 10% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg — and the high sugar and saturated fat content may further contribute to cardiovascular strain.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (76) and high sugar content (approximately 15.9 g sugar per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Choco Mochimochi donuts due to their high glycemic index of 76 and glycemic load of approximately 25 per serving, which will cause a rapid and significant postprandial blood glucose rise; if consumed, portion size must be tightly controlled and paired with low-GI foods.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (5.2 g per 100 g), trans fat (0.2 g per 100 g), and high sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume Choco Mochimochi donuts only occasionally, as the combination of saturated fat (~3.4 g per serving), trans fat (~0.1 g per serving), and high added sugar (~15.9 g per serving) may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels and overall cardiometabolic risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Choco Mochimochi is a chocolate-frosted mochi-style donut sold by Dunkin' Donuts in Singapore, including the Funan outlet. Unlike conventional Western cake donuts, the Mochimochi variant incorporates glutinous rice flour (mochiko) into the dough, producing a distinctively chewy, stretchy, and soft interior texture that contrasts with the smooth chocolate glaze coating the exterior. The donut is deep-fried and then dipped in a chocolate frosting. The flavour profile combines the mild sweetness of the mochi dough with the rich bitterness of cocoa-based chocolate glaze. Nutritionally, it is an ultra-processed snack food with approximately 391 kcal per 100 g, driven by its high carbohydrate content (~51 g per 100 g) and fat content (~19.5 g per 100 g). The glycemic index is high (~76), making it a food that causes rapid blood glucose elevation — a significant concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The sodium content (~338 mg per 100 g) is moderate and relevant for hypertension management. The saturated fat and trans fat content are relevant for cardiovascular disease risk. A typical serving is one donut (~65 g), providing approximately 254 kcal, 33 g carbohydrate, and 25 g glycemic load. This product is sold at SGD 2.90 per unit at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore outlets.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Choco Mochimochi donut?
Is Choco Mochimochi suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of a mochi donut?
Dunkin Donuts Singapore mochi donut nutrition
Choco Mochimochi carbs and sugar content
What makes a mochi donut chewy?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Choco Mochimochi exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Foods, or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from the closest USDA SR Legacy analogue (chocolate-frosted cake doughnut, NDB 18249) with adjustments for glutinous rice flour composition. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cake-type doughnuts. Confidence is medium because the structural analogy is strong and defensible, but exact product-level data from the manufacturer is not available.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy NDB 18249 (chocolate-frosted cake doughnut) as the closest approved-source analogue. Minor compositional adjustments made to reflect the higher glutinous rice flour content of mochi-style donuts. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. 3. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (cake-type doughnut); no direct GI measurement for mochi donuts is available in approved sources. Glutinous rice flour GI data from the University of Sydney database (GI ~86–98 for glutinous rice) supports the high GI classification. 4. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (76 × 33.28 g carbohydrate per 65 g serving) / 100 = 25.3, reported as 25.4. 5. Halal status is assumed based on Dunkin' Donuts Singapore's general halal certification; this should be verified against the current MUIS halal certificate. 6. The cuisine classification in the input ('Coffee') reflects the field survey platform tag and has been corrected to 'Donut / Bakery' as the canonical cuisine type. 7. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this product is sold in Singapore. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy NDB 18249 and are approximate. 9. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy NDB 18249 — chocolate-frosted cake doughnuts contain no vitamin C.
